gold.rationale: The source material only supports that Yang Zi's studio objected to unauthorized AI face-swapped/deep-synthesized videos and that the related videos were taken down after complaints. The article goes far beyond that record by inventing a platform-backed commercial rollout narrative and using synthetic or polished visuals as if they documented an official release.

atoms: A vertical screenshot of a short-video app interface featuring a female character in historical costume.
Large golden calligraphy text reads '花笺记' (Hua Jian Ji) on the left side.
Smaller vertical text below the title reads '快意恩仇 · 宿命难逃' (Fast revenge · Fate is hard to escape).
The female character has an elaborate updo with gold hair ornaments and a long tassel.
The character wears a sheer, embroidered white and gold robe.
A red heart icon with '28.6万' (286,000) below it indicates likes.
A comment bubble icon with '1.2万' (12,000) below it indicates comments.
A star icon with '3.0万' (30,000) below it indicates favorites.
A text box at the bottom left reads '第1集 | 全80集 · 已更新' (Episode 1 | Total 80 episodes · Updated).
A user handle '@花笺记官方' (Hua Jian Ji Official) is visible.
A 'Hot Trending TOP3' badge with '98.3万人' (9.83 million people watching) is at the bottom.
A search icon is in the top right corner.
A screenshot of a Weibo post from the account '杨紫工作室' (Yang Zi Studio).
The post timestamp is '26-3-20 16:33' and location is '北京' (Beijing).
The text explicitly mentions 'AI换脸' (AI face swap) and '深度合成' (deep synthesis).
The text states the studio has entrusted lawyers to investigate and pursue legal action.
The text mentions that the related videos have been taken down ('已下架').
A blue verification badge is next to the studio name.
A 'Public Welfare' (公益) tag is visible in the top right corner.
A screenshot of a video player interface showing a female character in distress.
The title overlay reads '重生后 我成了娘亲的守护神' (After Rebirth, I Became My Mother's Guardian Deity).
The episode number is 'EP14' with the subtitle '逆天改命' (Defying Fate).
The character has visible makeup tears and a red mark on her forehead.
A 'Login to get money' (登录领钱) button is on the right side.
A comment section shows a user saying '哭的时候好像杨紫' (Looks like Yang Zi when crying).
A disclaimer at the bottom left reads '作者声明：内容由AI生成' (Author Statement: Content generated by AI).
A 'Official Exclusive' (官方独播) badge is in the bottom right.
A screenshot of a customer service chat interface.
The header shows '在线客服' (Online Customer Service).
A system message instructs the user to submit complaint materials to 'hongguojubao@bytedance.com'.
The user asks '现在这个短剧搜不到了，是什么情况' (I can't find this short drama now, what's going on?).
The customer service replies '可能下架了哦' (It might have been taken down).
A small screenshot of the video player is attached to the top of the chat.
A watermark '南都娱乐' (Nandu Entertainment) is in the bottom right corner.
An infographic titled 'The Clone Economy' featuring a woman with a wireframe mesh overlay on her face.
A 'SIMILARITY SCORE' of '98.7%' is displayed prominently.
A section labeled 'DEEFAKE GENERATION' compares 'SOURCE FOOTAGE' with 'AI GENERATED' frames.
Waveform visualizations for 'VIDEO WAVEFORM', 'AUDIO WAVEFORM', and 'LIP SYNC ACCURACY' are visible.
A diagram at the bottom outlines 'THE AI CLONING PIPELINE' with steps: Data Collection, Model Training, Simulation, Rendering, Distribution.
A smartphone mockup on the right shows an app interface named 'STARCLONE' with a 'LIVE' indicator.
The phone screen displays 'EXCLUSIVE SERIES' with thumbnails of the same woman.
Text on the phone screen mentions 'FAN INTERACTIONS' and 'Live Q&A with your digital idol'.
A duplicate of the customer service chat screenshot from image 4.
The system message instructs to email 'hongguojubao@bytedance.com' for complaint materials.
The user asks about the short drama being unavailable.
The customer service replies '可能下架了哦' (It might have been taken down).
The chat interface has a 'Online Customer Service' header.
A watermark '南都娱乐' (Nandu Entertainment) is in the bottom right corner.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A promotional screenshot for a short drama app showing a female character in ancient Chinese attire. The interface includes engagement metrics like likes, comments, and favorites, along with episode information and trending status.
visual_indicators.ocr_text: 新剧推荐 | 关注 | 发现 | 推荐 | 花笺记 | 快意恩仇 · 宿命难逃 | 第1集 | 全80集 · 已更新 | @花笺记官方 | 每日12:00更新2集 | 会员抢先看 | 热播榜TOP3 · 98.3万人 | 28.6万 | 1.2万 | 3.0万
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The text describes a short drama release with high engagement, matching the visual layout of a popular app interface.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A formal statement from Yang Zi's official Weibo studio regarding unauthorized use of her likeness. The text details the legal action being taken against the infringing content.
visual_indicators.ocr_text: 杨紫工作室 | 当事方 | 26-3-20 16:33 | 演员杨紫工作室官博 | 发布于 北京 | 针对某作品未经授权，利用AI换脸、深度合成等技术，擅自使用杨紫女士的肖像制作并传播视频内容的行为，严重侵犯杨紫女士合法权益。工作室已委托律师对侵权内容进行取证，并将通过法律途径追究到底！目前相关视频已下架。技术可以创造美好未来，但不该成为侵权的工具。网络不是法外之地，工作室坚决维护艺人权益，绝不姑息任何侵权行为！
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The text confirms the studio's objection to AI face-swapping and deep synthesis, aligning with the context of the dispute.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A screenshot of a video player showing a dramatic scene with a female character. The interface includes episode details, a 'Login to get money' button, and a specific disclaimer about AI generation.
visual_indicators.ocr_text: 第14集 | 重生后 我成了娘亲的守护神 | EP14 逆天改命 | 登录领钱 | 3.7万 | 177 | 981 | 639 | 作者声明：内容由AI生成 | 官方独播 | 画质增强 | 热评 | 哭的时候好像杨紫
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The 'Author Statement: Content generated by AI' text directly contradicts the claim of a legitimate production, highlighting the controversy.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A chat log between a user and customer service regarding the removal of a short drama. The customer service provides an email address for further complaints.
visual_indicators.ocr_text: 在线客服 | 平台已收到你反馈的情况，为方便平台核实相关问题，请按如下投诉材料标准补齐投诉材料并提交至邮箱hongguojubao@bytedance.com。平台将在收到你的完整投诉材料后及时核查处理。感谢你的关注与支持！ | 现在这个短剧搜不到了，是什么情况 | 可能下架了哦 | 还有其他可以帮到你的吗~ | 南都娱乐
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The chat confirms the video was taken down ('下架') and provides contact info for the platform (Bytedance).

visual_indicators.scene_description: An infographic explaining the 'Clone Economy' and AI cloning pipeline. It features a woman with a facial mesh overlay and technical diagrams of deepfake generation.
visual_indicators.ocr_text: COVER STORY | The Clone Economy | Inside Hollywood's rush to build—and brand—AI versions of stars. | FACIAL LANDMARK MAPPING | SIMILARITY SCORE 98.7% | MODEL CONFIDENCE HIGH | DEEFAKE GENERATION | SOURCE FOOTAGE | AI GENERATED | VIDEO WAVEFORM | AUDIO WAVEFORM | LIP SYNC ACCURACY 96% | MODEL PARAMETERS | THE AI CLONING PIPELINE | DATA COLLECTION | MODEL TRAINING | SIMULATION | RENDERING | DISTRIBUTION | STARCLONE | LIVE | EXCLUSIVE SERIES | ECHOES Ep. 1 | ECHOES Ep. 2 | ECHOES Ep. 3 | BEHIND THE SCENES | FAN INTERACTIONS | Live Q&A with your digital idol | Join Live
visual_indicators.image_text_alignment_hint: The infographic illustrates the technical process of creating AI clones, supporting the narrative of how such content is produced.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A duplicate of the customer service chat log from image 4, showing the same exchange about the video being taken down.

retrieved_evidence.text: 【#擅自使用杨紫肖像的AI短剧已下架#】#曝AI短剧使用杨紫的脸#，对此@杨紫 工作室发声：针对某作品未经授权，利用AI换脸、深度合成等技术，擅自使用杨紫肖像制作并传播视频内容的行为，严重侵犯其合法权益。工作室已委托律师对侵权内容进行取证，并将通过法律途径追究到底！目前相关视频已下架。“技术 ​​​

Yang Zi’s studio issued a statement in recent days objecting to the use of the actress’s likeness in AI face-swapped or other deeply synthesized video content, according to a notice circulated on Chinese social media. The studio said it had discovered online clips in which Yang’s face was digitally overlaid onto unrelated scenes without authorization, and described such practices as damaging to her image. Representatives indicated that they are collecting evidence and may seek to hold relevant producers or distributors accountable through legal channels, while further details of potential actions remain under discussion.

#杨紫工作室回应AI换脸杨紫##红果回应有AI短剧使用杨紫的脸# 今日，#曝AI短剧使用杨紫的脸# 引发广泛关注。媒体就此事向红果平台核实，平台客服表示已收到相关反馈，目前涉事视频已下架处理。
随后@杨紫工作室 发布声明，明确此类AI换脸行为已严重侵犯杨紫合法权益，工作室已委托律师取证，并将通过法 ​​​

Yang Zi’s studio, which manages the actress’s commercial and publicity affairs, issued a public statement condemning the unauthorized use of AI face-swapping and so‑called deep synthesis technologies involving her appearance. In the text, highlighted by Chinese entertainment media, the studio argued that these practices had infringed Yang Zi’s lawful rights and personal dignity, stressing that the dispute went beyond ordinary fan-made edits and touched on emerging problems in the regulation of algorithmically produced performers on large short-video platforms.

The controversy surrounding the alleged misuse of actress Yang Zi’s image in AI-related video clips has been characterized by her studio as a matter of infringement of lawful rights, according to a statement released this week. The studio emphasized that any unauthorized use of her portrait, name or identity for commercial or widely disseminated content could violate personality and related rights under existing regulations. It called on producers and platforms to strengthen review mechanisms and urged users to refrain from forwarding questionable materials, while saying that relevant legal procedures may be pursued if necessary.

Commentaries in Chinese entertainment media have pointed out that the available statements from Yang Zi’s studio refer only to unauthorized use of her likeness in specific video works, rather than confirming any broader platform-produced drama franchise, according to recent reports. Analysts noted that references in the dispute focus on individual clips or short videos circulated online, and there has been no official documentation indicating that a full series or branded program involving AI-modified versions of the actress has been formally developed. Observers say further information from platforms would be needed to clarify the scope of the content involved.

Customer-service records cited in recent coverage showed that when Yang Zi’s representatives or fans attempted to locate the disputed micro-drama, platform support staff confirmed that the title could no longer be found in the catalog. These chat transcripts indicated the complaint had been registered but that the content was already unavailable, prompting legal experts and commentators to question how broadly the AI-derived series might have been distributed prior to removal and what internal review procedures the short-video platform had applied.

Media graphics and expert commentary on the Yang Zi case have depicted a streamlined industrial pipeline in which a celebrity-style portrait can be ingested by an AI face-mapping interface and then deployed across a mobile drama dashboard for serialized micro-dramas. Rights specialists noted that the combination of realistic facial cloning, automated costume generation and app-based distribution allows platforms to repurpose recognizable likenesses into low-cost, AI-driven content at scale, intensifying debates over portrait rights, consent and revenue-sharing in China’s entertainment sector.

Industry observers reviewing public statements related to the Yang Zi image dispute say the material released so far does not indicate that the actress herself has participated in, endorsed, licensed or negotiated any AI-driven project. According to summaries circulated by legal and entertainment commentators this week, the studio’s position centers on opposition to the unauthorized use of her likeness, without mentioning any cooperation frameworks. Analysts suggest that, in the absence of explicit contracts or endorsements, speculation about direct involvement by the performer should be treated with caution.

International observers watching developments in China’s AI and entertainment regulation have cited the Yang Zi case as a prominent example of how generative technologies intersect with star-driven media economies. Comparative analyses link the controversy to global debates on deepfake performers, synthetic influencers and digital doubles, arguing that short-video platforms’ experiments with AI micro-dramas will likely shape emerging standards for consent, attribution and monetization involving the digital personas of actors and other public figures.

Industry accounts examining the case of Yang Zi emphasized that the contested fantasy micro-drama was not merely circulating as a casual user creation but appeared within the short-video app’s vertical interface in a format indistinguishable from premium episodic releases. The series layout, episode labeling and promotional banners suggested a standardized, officially curated program featuring a lead character modeled on Yang Zi’s facial features, reinforcing concerns that the platform had integrated an unauthorized digital stand‑in into its conventional content pipeline.

Several Chinese entertainment news outlets and popular social media accounts reported this week that the video materials at the center of the recent dispute involving actress Yang Zi had been removed from major platforms. According to these accounts, links shared by users now display error messages or are no longer publicly accessible, suggesting that platform operators or content uploaders may have taken them down. Commentators noted that the specific timing and the party initiating the removal have not been clearly disclosed, and follow-up measures by regulators or rights holders remain to be seen.

Technologists involved in AI development note that modern face-swapping and generative video systems can now create highly convincing replicas of public figures such as Yang Zi using relatively small training datasets. In discussions about the short-video platform case, they emphasize that user-friendly interfaces have lowered technical barriers, making it easy for producers and studios to test different celebrity likenesses in micro-dramas, which in turn raises complex questions about accountability when platform branding suggests official endorsement.

Recent discussions among media professionals and legal analysts in China suggest that publicly available information surrounding the Yang Zi incident does not substantiate claims of a formal platform strategy to launch AI-based celebrity replicas. According to these commentators, the existing statements do not describe an official commercial rollout, revenue-sharing arrangements or a large-scale market plan linked to AI-generated versions of performers. Instead, the focus of the dispute appears confined to a limited number of videos alleged to have used the actress’s likeness without permission, with broader commercial implications remaining unclear.

In recent reports on China’s booming short-video industry, a major mobile streaming platform was described as promoting a Chinese-style fantasy micro-drama built around a heroine closely resembling actress Yang Zi, dressed in ornate historical costume and shown within a familiar app-style interface. Observers noted that the work was marketed with a glossy, platform-branded release aesthetic typical of official serial content, raising concerns that an advanced algorithmic digital replica of Yang Zi’s likeness had been used without authorization as part of a broader commercial push for face-cloned entertainment characters.

Within the broader entertainment ecosystem, Chinese short-video providers have aggressively promoted micro-dramas and vertical series as new revenue drivers, often blending human performances with algorithmically generated backdrops and characters. Analysts observing the Yang Zi dispute suggest that the commercial incentive to create instantly familiar digital leads can encourage the replication of popular actors’ faces, and that without clear contractual frameworks, this dynamic risks normalizing unlicensed face-cloning as a routine production technique.

Consumer advocates responding to the Yang Zi incident have highlighted the perspective of ordinary viewers who encounter AI-based micro-dramas within heavily gamified short-video apps. According to recent commentary, the seamless integration of digital replicas into fantasy series, recommendation feeds and branded release pages can make it difficult for audiences to distinguish between authorized collaborations and unlicensed cloning, underscoring calls for clearer on-screen disclosures and stronger penalties for misuse of celebrity likenesses.

Legal scholars examining the controversy surrounding Yang Zi and Chinese short-video platforms have pointed to China’s evolving regulatory framework on deep synthesis technologies, including provisions that require prominent labeling of algorithmically produced content and explicit consent for the commercial use of an individual’s likeness. Commentators argue that the incident illustrates gaps between written rules and platform-level enforcement, with the rapid growth of AI micro-dramas challenging existing mechanisms for rights protection and complaint resolution.

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post accurately reports the core event (studio objection and video takedown) but significantly inflates the narrative by asserting a structured, commercial 'AI drama' rollout and specific series titles not supported by the evidence.
text_analysis.t2_evidence: The post claims the incident was a 'failed trial run in platform-supported synthetic entertainment' and describes a 'structured content rollout' with a specific title 'Reborn, I Became My Mother's Guardian Deity', whereas evidence [5] and [8] explicitly state there is 'no official documentation indicating that a full series... has been formally developed' and the dispute focuses on 'individual clips or short videos' without evidence of a formal franchise.
t2_problematic_subclaims: The incident was a 'failed trial run in platform-supported synthetic entertainment' and a 'structured content rollout'.
The specific title 'Reborn, I Became My Mother's Guardian Deity' was linked to the controversy as a micro-drama.
The release felt like a 'proper platform debut' rather than a fan edit.
text_analysis.global_assessment: The post accurately reports the studio's legal objection and the subsequent takedown but dangerously overstates the existence of a formal, commercial AI drama franchise that the evidence suggests was never officially confirmed.

images.scene_description: A promotional poster for a short drama titled 'Flower Record' featuring a woman in historical costume with elaborate gold hair accessories, presented within a mobile app interface.

images.explanation: The image is a synthetic press-style poster matching the description of promotional material for the AI drama, confirming the V1 label due to its polished, non-candid visual style.

images.scene_description: A screenshot of a Weibo post from 'Yang Zi Studio' containing a formal statement about legal action against unauthorized AI use.

image_analysis.global_notes: The post uses a mix of synthetic press-style posters (V1) and genuine screenshots (V2 NO) to tell the story of the AI drama controversy, with the V1 images serving as the primary visual evidence for the disputed content.

judge.level3_rationale: 该文章虽然准确报道了杨紫工作室对AI换脸短剧的抗议及视频下架事件，但通过虚构‘结构化内容发布’的叙事，错误地将图片中的《花笺记》指认为《重生后，我成了娘亲的守护神》，并混入了AI克隆经济的概念图，导致受众对事件的具体细节和性质产生严重误解。
judge.reasoning_trace: The post combines a true core event (studio objection) with a fabricated narrative (specific drama title and 'structured rollout') and misleading visuals (wrong drama title in image 1, AI infographic in image 5). The T2 distortion and C1/C2 mismatches prevent a 'True' or 'Mostly True' rating, while the presence of V1 (infographic) and the severity of the misattribution push the verdict to 'Mostly False'.
judge.core_claim: The post describes a specific incident where a short-video platform released an AI-generated micro-drama featuring a woman resembling Yang Zi, which was subsequently taken down after her studio objected to the unauthorized use of her likeness.
key_subclaims: The disputed video was titled 'Reborn, I Became My Mother's Guardian Deity' (重生后，我成了娘亲的守护神).
The video was presented as a structured, commercial platform release with high engagement metrics.
The studio issued a statement on March 20 regarding unauthorized AI face-swapping.
The video was removed from the platform following the objection.
The images show a specific drama titled 'Flower Record' (花笺记) featuring a different actress than Yang Zi.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The disputed video was titled 'Reborn, I Became My Mother's Guardian Deity'.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence suggests this title was linked to the controversy in online discussion, but the post presents it as the definitive title of the 'structured content rollout'.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The video was presented as a structured, commercial platform release.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence confirms the content appeared in a format indistinguishable from premium releases, but the post exaggerates this into a 'failed trial run' and 'semi-formal AI release'.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The studio issued a statement on March 20 regarding unauthorized AI face-swapping.
subclaim_findings.note: Confirmed by the Weibo post screenshot and social media reports.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The video was removed from the platform following the objection.
subclaim_findings.note: Confirmed by the customer service chat log and studio statement.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The images show a specific drama titled 'Flower Record' (花笺记) featuring a woman resembling Yang Zi.
subclaim_findings.note: The image shows a drama titled 'Hua Jian Ji' (花笺记) with a character who does not appear to be Yang Zi, contradicting the post's implication that the images are from the specific 'Reborn' drama featuring Yang Zi.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The post implies the 'Reborn' drama is the primary example of the 'Clone Economy'.
subclaim_findings.note: The infographic 'The Clone Economy' is a conceptual illustration, not a screenshot of the specific drama.

judge.verdict_reasoning: The post fabricates a specific narrative about a 'structured content rollout' and misidentifies the specific drama title in the images (claiming 'Reborn' while showing 'Hua Jian Ji'). The inclusion of the 'Clone Economy' infographic (V1) and the misattribution of the specific drama title (C1/C2) creates a misleading picture of the event. The core claim about the studio's objection is true, but the framing and visual evidence are distorted.

image_findings.explanation: The image shows a drama titled 'Hua Jian Ji' (花笺记), which contradicts the post's claim that the images are from the specific 'Reborn' drama featuring Yang Zi. The character in the image is not Yang Zi, creating a semantic mismatch between the image and the text's specific narrative about the 'Reborn' drama.
The Weibo post screenshot accurately reflects the studio's statement about unauthorized AI use and the video takedown.
The image is an AI-generated clip (as indicated by the text 'Content generated by AI') featuring a character resembling Yang Zi, which aligns with the post's description of the disputed content.
The customer service chat log confirms the video was taken down, matching the post's narrative.
The infographic illustrates the 'Clone Economy' concept, which is consistent with the post's broader analysis of the industry trend.
Duplicate of image 4, confirming the platform's response.
